steps.title: Combine terms with a on the left
steps.content: Start with $4a = \frac{9b^2}{a} - 12a$. Add $12a$ to both sides to get $4a + 12a = \frac{9b^2}{a}$, which simplifies to $16a = \frac{9b^2}{a}$.

steps.title: Clear the denominator
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $a$ to obtain $16a^2 = 9b^2$.

steps.title: Isolate a and take the square root
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 16:
$$a^2 = \frac{9b^2}{16}$$
Take the positive square root (since $a \gt 0$):
$$a = \sqrt{\frac{9b^2}{16}} = \frac{3b}{4}$$
That matches Choice C — $a=\frac{3b}{4}$.
